metallic targets have been prepared by the classical
vacuum evaporation technique.

end of irradiations.  The samples were assayed for two
or three weeks for medium half-lives activities and
then regularly during two or three months for
longer-lived isotopes.

-By authors. The thick target between thick catchers
technique has not been used for the natural Cu-target,
nevertheless, summing partial cross sections in thin
catchers and in target, an estimation of the total
cross-section has been made.
- By compiler. The authors did not distinguish
cumulative and direct productons.

ISABELLE and EVA codes.  The mass-yield curve is
Reasonably well reproduced by the theoretical approach
for the high mass fragments but it exists a large
discrepancy for the lightest residues.
SD: SF5=CHN added to REACTION code.
Year of ref. in REL-REF corrected.
Figures 5,6.
For the angular distributions measurements, the thin
target, 200-400-mg/cm**2 thickness, was placed at
45-degree against the beam axis. The recoil nuclei were
stopped in angular 100-mum MYLAR catcher foils arranged
inside the chamber. In several angular distribution
measurements, the 100-mum MYLAR foil was replaced by
one MYLAR layers associated with an Al-layer to
discriminate different kinetic energies at different
angles for a given final product. In other experiments,
at a mean of 30-degree, the 100-mum thick MYLAR
collector has been replaced by a series of 3.5-mum
thick MYLAR catchers able to give range estimations at
a given angular domain.
The uncertainty is given by authors on
figure. An analysis is not given.
